title: Air Fryer Smashed Potatoes
tagline: Boiled, smashed, blasted. Maximum crust per potato.
description: Baby potatoes boiled until creamy, smashed flat, and air-fried until the edges shatter. The smash creates crags; the air fryer turns every crag into crust. The best potato-to-effort ratio there is.
ingredients: 2 Tbsp Diamond Crystal kosher salt (for the boiling water)
Flaky salt and chopped parsley or chives, to finish
steps: Cover potatoes with cold water by an inch, add the 2 Tbsp salt, and boil until a fork slides in with zero resistance, 15-18 minutes. Undercooked potatoes don't smash, they break.
Drain and let them steam-dry in the colander 5 minutes - surface moisture is the enemy of crust.
On a cutting board, press each potato to about 1/2 inch thick with the bottom of a glass or measuring cup. Ragged edges are the goal.
Mix olive oil, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Brush or spoon over both sides of each smashed potato - get into the crags.
Air fry at 400°F (200°C) in a single layer (batches if needed), 15-18 minutes, flipping once halfway, until deep golden and crackling at the edges.
Finish with flaky salt and herbs. Serve hot - the crunch has a half-life of about 15 minutes.
notes: Yukon Golds are creamy inside; red potatoes work, russets fall apart.
Salting the boil like pasta water seasons the potato all the way through - the only chance you get.
Oven version: 450°F on a preheated sheet pan, 25-30 minutes, same flip.
testedNote: Fork-tender before smashing and a real steam-dry are the two non-negotiables.

verdict.note: The correct answer to 'what do I make with these baby potatoes' from now on.

troubleshooting: Potatoes fell apart when smashed → overboiled or pressed too thin; fork-tender, half an inch.
No crunch → crowded basket or wet potatoes; single layer, steam-dry first.
Stuck to the basket → dry basket; the oil goes on the potatoes AND a light mist on the basket.
Bland → seasoned only at the end; the salt in the boiling water is doing the real work.
